name: footer-1
text: Robotics & Embedded Systems Club  |  1/6
About 20 seconds. Open by saying that the club connects learning and building: we teach microcontrollers first, then turn those skills into projects that solve visible problems.

name: slide-title
text: What Our Club Does

name: intro-lead
text: We build a bridge from basic electronics lessons to complete, testable prototypes.
textPreview: We build a bridge from basic electronics lessons to complete, testable prototypes.

name: intro-body
text: So far, our foundation work has focused on microcontroller programming, sensor input, motor/output control, simple debugging, and turning a circuit idea into a physical system.
textPreview: So far, our foundation work has focused on microcontroller programming, sensor input, motor/output control, simple debugging, and turning...

text:
Board setup

name: card-title-Arduino Basics
text: Arduino Basics

name: card-body-Arduino Basics
text:
Sensor reading
Relay/output control
Prototype wiring
Real-world automation
textPreview: Sensor reading | Relay/output control | Prototype wiring | Real-world automation

name: footer-2
text: Robotics & Embedded Systems Club  |  2/6

About 25 seconds. Explain the club simply: not just lectures, not just competitions. The goal is to let members learn a platform, build a circuit, test it, and then show a working outcome. This sets up Cindy's Arduino project.

name: slide-title
text: Project 1: Cindy's Automatic Watering System

name: project-lead
text: The Arduino lecture becomes a useful daily-life prototype.
textPreview: The Arduino lecture becomes a useful daily-life prototype.

name: project-body
text: It reads soil moisture under the surface and only waters when moisture falls below the normal threshold. The design helps plants survive during long trips, reduces difficulty for older people, and avoids wasting water in everyday irrigation.
textPreview: It reads soil moisture under the surface and only waters when moisture falls below the normal threshold. The design helps plants survive ...

name: card-title-Process
text: Process

name: card-body-Process
text:
Built the main circuit
Connected sensor and pump control
Ran field tests and experiments
textPreview: Built the main circuit | Connected sensor and pump control | Ran field tests and experiments

name: card-title-Target Outcome
text: Target Outcome

name: card-body-Target Outcome
text:
About 30% less water than manual watering
Runs with very little intervention
Manual adjustment no more than once per week
textPreview: About 30% less water than manual watering | Runs with very little intervention | Manual adjustment no more than once per week

name: footer-3
text: Robotics & Embedded Systems Club  |  3/6

About 35 seconds. Say this project shows why the Arduino lesson matters. The key idea is not watering on a timer; it waters only when the soil needs it. Mention the expected 30 percent water saving and weekly-or-less manual adjustment target.

title: Project 2: From STM32 Board to Robot Body

name: slide-title
text: Project 2: From STM32 Board to Robot Body
textPreview: Project 2: From STM32 Board to Robot Body

name: robot-summary
text: Outcome so far: a grounded robot workflow from firmware, through ROS 2 command topics, to physical mechanical parts.
textPreview: Outcome so far: a grounded robot workflow from firmware, through ROS 2 command topics, to physical mechanical parts.

name: card-title-Controller
text: Controller

name: card-body-Controller
text: Started from STM32-style board practice and embedded control fundamentals.
textPreview: Started from STM32-style board practice and embedded control fundamentals.

name: card-title-Firmware
text: Firmware

name: card-body-Firmware
text: Lower-controller code integrates motors, IMU attitude estimation, SBUS input, and serial communication.
textPreview: Lower-controller code integrates motors, IMU attitude estimation, SBUS input, and serial communication.

name: card-title-Robot System
text: Robot System

name: card-body-Robot System
text: Iterated toward a wheel-foot robot with ROS 2 bridge, URDF/RViz visualization, and following logic.
textPreview: Iterated toward a wheel-foot robot with ROS 2 bridge, URDF/RViz visualization, and following logic.

name: footer-4
text: Robotics & Embedded Systems Club  |  4/6

About 35 seconds. Transition from Cindy's project: the same embedded thinking scales up. Start from a board, then firmware, then the robot body. Mention that the current resources include lower-controller firmware, ROS 2 base bridge, URDF/RViz assets, following-system code, and STL mechanical files.

title: Engineering Files: Parts That Become the Body

name: slide-title
text: Engineering Files: Parts That Become the Body
textPreview: Engineering Files: Parts That Become the Body

name: stl-intro
text: These STL files are not decorations. They are printable mechanical resources used to move from a controller demo toward a complete robot structure.
textPreview: These STL files are not decorations. They are printable mechanical resources used to move from a controller demo toward a complete robot ...

text: back_shell.stl

name: stl-takeaway
text: Show these as the bridge from digital design to physical assembly.
textPreview: Show these as the bridge from digital design to physical assembly.

name: footer-5
text: Robotics & Embedded Systems Club  |  5/6

About 30 seconds. Tell the audience these are the engineering files. Point to the back shell, bolt pin, and bracket. The important message is that the project is no longer only code; it has parts that can be printed, assembled, tested, and revised.

text: Final Outcome: Live Robot Demonstration

name: outcome-body
text:
A club learning path that produced working prototypes:

1. Microcontroller lectures
2. Cindy's Arduino watering system
3. A robot pipeline from firmware to body design

Next step: insert the final robot video here.

name: footer-6
text: Robotics & Embedded Systems Club  |  6/6

About 25 seconds plus the video. Close by saying the outcome is the process itself: members learned embedded systems, made a useful Arduino product, and started a more ambitious robot project. Then play or insert the final video manually.
